title: BUG: Remove method setting in summary2 of genmod
body:
- [X] closes #9315 
- [X] tests added / passed. 
- [X] code/documentation is well formatted.  
- [X] properly formatted commit message. See 
      [NumPy's guide](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/numpy-1.15.1/dev/gitwash/development_workflow.html#writing-the-commit-message). 

<details>


**Notes**:

* It is essential that you add a test when making code changes. Tests are not 
  needed for doc changes.
* When adding a new function, test values should usually be verified in another package (e.g., R/SAS/Stata).
* When fixing a bug, you must add a test that would produce the bug in main and
  then show that it is fixed with the new code.
* New code additions must be well formatted. Changes should pass flake8. If on Linux or OSX, you can
  verify you changes are well formatted by running 
  ```
  git diff upstream/main -u -- "*.py" | flake8 --diff --isolated
  ```
  assuming `flake8` is installed. This command is also available on Windows 
  using the Windows System for Linux once `flake8` is installed in the 
  local Linux environment. While passing this test is not required, it is good practice and it help 
  improve code quality in `statsmodels`.
* Docstring additions must render correctly, including escapes and LaTeX.

</details>

step_plan.summary: Fix genmod `summary2()` to be side-effect free by removing any assignment to the fitted results’ `method` attribute (in GLM and GEE results) while still reporting the method via a read-only lookup or local fallback.

steps.operation: Remove any mutation/assignment of the fitted result's `method` attribute during `summary2()` construction and instead use a local display value/fallback when populating the summary info
steps.reason: `summary2()` must be read-only/reporting; setting `self.method` (or otherwise mutating results state) while generating output can corrupt metadata and cause downstream behavior changes. If the summary needs a “Method” line, it should read an existing `res.method` set at fit time or use a local `getattr(res, 'method', '')` fallback without touching object state.
steps.side_effects: Summary output content should remain the same (or become more accurate), but if older behavior relied on `summary2()` to create a missing `method` attribute, some callers expecting that side-effect will stop seeing it. Requires running genmod test suite; may need minor adjustments if any tests implicitly depended on the mutation.

steps.operation: Remove any mutation/assignment of the fitted result's `method` attribute during `summary2()` construction and ensure the displayed “Method” uses an existing attribute or a non-mutating fallback
steps.reason: If GEE results follow the same pattern as GLM results, `summary2()` must not override or set `res.method` as part of formatting. The method used to fit should be established at fit time and treated as read-only during reporting.
steps.side_effects: If GEE previously papered over missing `method` by setting it inside `summary2()`, the summary may need an explicit fallback string for display. Run existing GEE summary tests and any doctests that include summary output.
